channel.title: Natural Language Processing on CEDIS
channel.description: Recent content in Natural Language Processing on CEDIS

item.title: Artificial Intelligence and Natural Language Processing
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a branch of computer science that seeks to develop systems capable of mimicking and enhancing human abilities such as reasoning, learning, and perception. One of its most fascinating subareas is Natural Language Processing (NLP).

item.title: Project DFCris
item.description: A research project in partnership with IBICT and funded by FAP/DF.

item.title: People
item.description: Full Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the undergraduate Software Engineering program and the Graduate Program in Applied Computing.

item.title: Llms for legal labor consultation an undergraduate thesis presents a multimodal chatbot
item.description: Álvaro Henrique de Sousa Gouvea and Luís Guilherme Gaboardi Lins defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis using Large Language Models to build a multimodal chatbot for legal-labor consultation.

item.title: Latte: a duo trains a gamified chatbot to help write scientific articles
item.description: Luiz Henrique Fernandes Zamprogno and Victor Eduardo Araújo Ribeiro defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis introducing Latte — a gamified chatbot to support the production of scientific articles.

item.title: What users say when no one asks: an undergraduate thesis mines Play Store reviews for usability signals
item.description: Augusto Moreno Vilarins Cardoso da Silva and Ícaro Pereira de Oliveira defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis designing a semi-automated process to extract usability signals from user comments on the Google Play Store.

item.title: Before the generative AI boom, an undergraduate thesis was already teaching machines to grade essays
item.description: Back in 2016, well before the LLM frenzy, Cristóvão Frinhani defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis that uses semantic similarity and machine learning to support distance-learning tutors grading essay questions.
